description:
## Context

`src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr` is the **largest source file** in the project at **797 lines** and mixes **at least 5 distinct concerns** in a single module:

1. Lazy-initialized semaphore / counter / repair mutex (lines ~33-110) — global concurrency primitives
2. Generic helpers: `cancel_check`, `log_duration_warning`, `best_available_feed`, `fallback_feed`, `interruptible_sleep`, `build_software_releases`, `build_tab_feeds` (lines ~112-180)
3. Per-feed concurrent fetch with timeout + fallback (lines ~180-300)
4. Refresh loop supervisor: `State` class + `check_stuck_recovery`, `check_semaphore_health`, `reload_config_if_changed`, `run_initial_refresh`, `run_timed_refresh`, `sleep_between_cycles`, `log_heartbeat`, `start` (lines ~310-720)
5. Periodic memory/health reporter `start_health_reporter` (lines ~480-520)
6. Backward-compat top-level method shims and alias (lines ~770-797)

It also contains **two `defensive` code paths whose own comments admit they cover code that does not exist**, **magic timeout numbers scattered through the file**, and a **duplicated `refresh_all` top-level definition** at the bottom.

## Why now

- This file is on the suspect list for the in-flight P0 memory-growth ticket `quickhea-alz` (manages fibers, channels, @@class state, and a long-lived `spawn`-loop).
- Readability: a 70-line `start` method with three levels of nested branching is hard to reason about and hard to test.
- Reliability: lazy-initialized class-level `@@` state is hard to test and reset; a stuck state can only be cleaned by reaching into internals.
- Cohesion: the existing convention in `src/fetcher/` is small focused files (`feed_fetcher.cr`, `fetcher_cache.cr`, `fetcher_favicon.cr`, `fetcher_response.cr`, `fetcher_retry.cr`, `vug_adapter.cr`, `refresh_health_monitor.cr`). `refresh_loop.cr` violates this convention.

## Non-goals

- No behavior change. The supervisor must still be invoked as `RefreshLoop.start(config_path, cache, db_service)`. The public API (`refresh_all`, `start`, `CancelError`, the `RefreshHealthMonitor` alias) must remain backward-compatible.
- Do NOT touch `quickhea-alz` (P0 memory growth) as part of this epic. If the refactor uncovers new evidence, file a follow-up ticket.

## Refactor strategy (split into child tickets)

1. `semaphore_pool.cr` — encapsulate concurrency primitives
2. `feed_fetcher_concurrent.cr` — extract per-feed concurrent fetch
3. `refresh_supervisor.cr` — extract `State` + orchestration helpers + `start`
4. `refresh_health_reporter.cr` — extract periodic memory/health reporter
5. Slim `refresh_loop.cr` down to public API + re-exports
6. Replace magic timeouts with named module constants
7. Remove dead "defense-in-depth" CancelError rescue + duplicate top-level `refresh_all` definitions

## Verification (must pass before closing the epic)

- `just nix-build` succeeds (frontend assets + Crystal binary)
- `nix develop . --command crystal spec` — all existing specs pass
- `cd frontend && npm run test` — frontend tests pass
- `bin/quickheadlines` boots; a refresh cycle runs end-to-end; `GET /api/health` returns healthy
- Manual smoke: kill an in-flight fetch, verify cancel path still works
title: Refactor src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr (797 lines) — SRP, readability, reliability
